rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly as core theoretical support for the mechanism linking preference uncertainty to context-dependent choice, especially compromise choices. In the literature review and hypothesis development, the authors explicitly invoke Dhar and Simonson (2003) to argue that compromise options are chosen to resolve uncertainty, then predict their own manipulation should reduce such choices. They return to this citation in Study 1 framing and in the general discussion as part of the foundational prior stream their contribution builds on.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Dhar and Simonson (2003) as a conceptual foundation for why preference uncertainty increases reliance on compromise-type context effects. It then introduces self-perceived attractiveness/self-confidence as a new antecedent that reduces uncertainty and attenuates those effects.
